description:
A DevOps colleague has asked you to turn on verbose logging for a small, non-privileged FTP-style service that lives entirely in your home directory and to extract the first batch of DEBUG entries for analysis.

SYSTEM LAYOUT
1. Configuration
   • /home/user/tinyftp/tinyftp.conf  
     - Current contents:  
       # tinyftp configuration  
       port=2121  
       log-level=INFO  

2. Logs
   • /home/user/logs/tinyftp.log – already populated with mixed INFO/DEBUG/ERROR lines.

3. Directories
   • /home/user/tinyftp/ (contains the config file)  
   • /home/user/logs/      (contains the current log)

WHAT YOU MUST DO
Step-by-step (the entire job can be solved with roughly 3–4 shell commands):

1. Safeguard the current configuration  
   • Create /home/user/tinyftp/tinyftp.conf.bak as an exact copy of the original tinyftp.conf before you modify anything.

2. Enable verbose (DEBUG) logging  
   • In /home/user/tinyftp/tinyftp.conf change the value of “log-level” from INFO to DEBUG.  
   • Do NOT alter any other lines or whitespace.

3. Harvest the existing DEBUG entries  
   • Read /home/user/logs/tinyftp.log and copy every line that contains the literal substring “DEBUG:” (case-sensitive) to a new file called /home/user/logs/tinyftp_debug.log, preserving the original ordering of those lines.

4. Produce a one-line summary  
   • Count how many lines you placed in tinyftp_debug.log and record that number in a new file named /home/user/logs/tinyftp_debug_summary.log.  
   • The summary file must contain exactly one line (followed by a single newline) in this format:  
     DEBUG_LINES_COUNT=<number>

5. Console confirmation  
   • When you are done, echo the same line (DEBUG_LINES_COUNT=<number>) to stdout so the requester can see the result immediately.

OUTPUT/FILE FORMAT REQUIREMENTS
• /home/user/tinyftp/tinyftp.conf  
  - Must now include the line “log-level=DEBUG” exactly once.  
• /home/user/tinyftp/tinyftp.conf.bak  
  - Must be byte-for-byte identical to the original, unedited configuration.  
• /home/user/logs/tinyftp_debug.log  
  - Must contain only the DEBUG lines (no blank lines, no extra text) in the order they appeared in the source log.  
• /home/user/logs/tinyftp_debug_summary.log  
  - Must contain exactly one line, terminated by a single newline:  
    DEBUG_LINES_COUNT=<integer>  
  - The integer must equal the number of lines in tinyftp_debug.log.

PERMISSIONS
All work must be done with the normal user account; no sudo is required or allowed. All mentioned paths are writeable by the current user.

TYPICAL COMMAND HINTS (do NOT copy verbatim)
• A copy command for the backup.  
• A stream editor or text substitution for the config tweak.  
• A grep or awk pipeline to isolate DEBUG lines.  
• A wc or similar utility (or shell variable) to compute the line count.

Once the backup, configuration edit, filtered log, summary file and console output are all in place exactly as described, the task is finished.
truth:
EXPECTED FINAL STATE (used by automated tests)

1. /home/user/tinyftp/tinyftp.conf  
   # tinyftp configuration  
   port=2121  
   log-level=DEBUG  
   (file ends with a single newline)

2. /home/user/tinyftp/tinyftp.conf.bak  
   # tinyftp configuration  
   port=2121  
   log-level=INFO  
   (file ends with a single newline)

3. /home/user/logs/tinyftp.log  
   (unchanged, already present before the task starts)  
   2023-09-01 12:00:00 INFO: Server started on port 2121  
   2023-09-01 12:01:00 DEBUG: Connection from 192.168.1.10 accepted  
   2023-09-01 12:02:00 ERROR: Failed login attempt for user 'john'  
   2023-09-01 12:03:00 DEBUG: Connection closed for 192.168.1.10  
   2023-09-01 12:04:00 INFO: Server status: OK  

4. /home/user/logs/tinyftp_debug.log  
   2023-09-01 12:01:00 DEBUG: Connection from 192.168.1.10 accepted  
   2023-09-01 12:03:00 DEBUG: Connection closed for 192.168.1.10  
   (file ends with a single newline)

5. /home/user/logs/tinyftp_debug_summary.log  
   DEBUG_LINES_COUNT=2  
   (file ends with a single newline)

6. Console (stdout) must contain a line that matches exactly:  
   DEBUG_LINES_COUNT=2

The automated grader will verify:
• Presence and byte-wise correctness of the backup file.  
• Correct modification of the live config file.  
• Exact contents of tinyftp_debug.log and tinyftp_debug_summary.log.  
• Correct stdout line.
